channel.title: NPR Programs: Morning Edition
channel.description: Morning Edition takes listeners around the country and the world with multi-faceted stories and commentaries every weekday. Hosts Steve Inskeep, Leila Fadel, Michel Martin and A Martínez bring you the latest breaking news and features to prepare you for the day.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: Fast walkers in their 80s cut their risk of cognitive decline by half, a study finds
item.description: A new study of people 80 and older with exceptional gaits finds fast walkers have about a 50% lower risk of cognitive decline, showing the connection between physical health and brain health.
item.content:encoded:
A new study of people 80 and older with exceptional gaits finds fast walkers have about a 50% lower risk of cognitive decline, showing the connection between physical health and brain health.

(Image credit: FG Trade)
item.dc:creator: Allison Aubrey

item.title: Democrats and Republicans pitch different visions of America on July 4th
item.description: Both parties used the Fourth of July holiday to pitch their vision for the country.
item.content:encoded: Both parties used the Fourth of July holiday to pitch their vision for the country.
item.dc:creator: Mara Liasson

item.title: How Trump marked the 250th anniversary of independence
item.description: NPR's Michel Martin speaks to Sarah Isgur, senior editor at The Dispatch, about President Trump's celebration of the 250th anniversary of the Declaration of Independence.
item.content:encoded: NPR's Michel Martin speaks to Sarah Isgur, senior editor at The Dispatch, about President Trump's celebration of the 250th anniversary of the Declaration of Independence.
item.dc:creator: Michel Martin

item.title: Government launches Trump Accounts, offering $1,000 to eligible babies
item.description: The federal government has launched new tax-deferred investment accounts called Trump Accounts, offering $1,000 of seed money for eligible newborns.
item.content:encoded: The federal government has launched new tax-deferred investment accounts called Trump Accounts, offering $1,000 of seed money for eligible newborns.
item.dc:creator: Hosts

item.title: France is sweltering after hottest June in decades
item.description: France recorded its hottest June since records began in 1947, as a deadly heat wave drove temperatures above 104 degrees Fahrenheit (40 degrees Celsius) across much of the country.
item.content:encoded: France recorded its hottest June since records began in 1947, as a deadly heat wave drove temperatures above 104 degrees Fahrenheit (40 degrees Celsius) across much of the country.
item.dc:creator: Eleanor Beardsley

item.title: Philip Glass' new symphony premieres at Tanglewood after Kennedy Center cancellation
item.description: Philip Glass' newest symphony, an homage to Abraham Lincoln, was supposed to premiere at the Kennedy Center — until it didn't. And then, the Boston Symphony Orchestra stepped in.
item.content:encoded:
Philip Glass' newest symphony, an homage to Abraham Lincoln, was supposed to premiere at the Kennedy Center — until it didn't. And then, the Boston Symphony Orchestra stepped in.

(Image credit: Hilary Scott)
item.dc:creator: Anastasia Tsioulcas

item.title: Ultra-Orthodox Jews are protesting against serving in Israel's army
item.description: Long exempt from military service, some ultra-Orthodox Jews are serving in Israel's army, while others are arrested for dodging the draft and many more block highways in protest.
item.content:encoded: Long exempt from military service, some ultra-Orthodox Jews are serving in Israel's army, while others are arrested for dodging the draft and many more block highways in protest.
item.dc:creator: Itay Stern

item.title: Former Marine says military veterans running for office advance bipartisan politics
item.description: A former Marine says in a new book that military veterans running for office can bring Washington the courage to work across party lines.
item.content:encoded: A former Marine says in a new book that military veterans running for office can bring Washington the courage to work across party lines.
item.dc:creator: Quil Lawrence

item.title: In Philly neighborhood known for drug crime, soccer club offers kids hope
item.description: The Kensington neighborhood in Philadelphia has long drawn national attention as one of America's biggest open-air criminal drug markets. Amid the crime and poverty, activists have also created a thriving soccer culture, where World Cup passion is fierce.
item.content:encoded: The Kensington neighborhood in Philadelphia has long drawn national attention as one of America's biggest open-air criminal drug markets. Amid the crime and poverty, activists have also created a thriving soccer culture, where World Cup passion is fierce.
item.dc:creator: Brian Mann

item.title: Some voters in Louisville, Kentucky, got wrong ballots for years
item.description: Some voters in Louisville. Kentucky have been voting given the wrong ballots in elections for several years. That's the result of an investigation by Louisville Public Media
item.content:encoded: Some voters in Louisville. Kentucky have been voting given the wrong ballots in elections for several years. That's the result of an investigation by Louisville Public Media
item.dc:creator: Justin Hicks
